rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF ERMA L. ADAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/19/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. (Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.]) Was there an order waiving notice to the conservatee? (Probate Code Section 1460(a)(2).] (2) There does not appear to be a post-move notice filed for the record, although Conservatee now resides at another facility. (3) There were two deposits of unknown source, one quite significant. Did the conservator not follow up with the bank? (4) Why was $1,250 in interest reversed by (to?) Poppy Bank on 1/17/23? (5) The Poppy Bank checking account is still in the conservatee's name. (6) No statements were filed to verify the ending balances in the D.A Davidson account xx4057 or the Genworth Life Insurance policy. (7) The D.A. Davidson account xx6034 has a slightly different ending value than shown in the accounting, and no reconciliation is shown. (8) Under what authority did the conservator purchase securities? [Probate Code Section 2570.] (9) There was an order signed on 12/18/23 reducing bond to $986,000. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/5/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Transfers of cash between accounts are no changes in the FORM of assets.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTONIA H. SANCHEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/19/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Paragraph 21 indicates that there was no cash in the estate. The Corrected I&A filed on 5/19/25 reported $5,796.78 cash on hand as of the date of death, and there is reportedly still some cash on hand. (3) Paragraph 28 of the petition indicates that the estate has a 5/30th interest in the property in LA with the APN 3075-008-001, but the I&A reported a 7/30th interest. (4) It appears that this petition is premature, if the predeceased sister's estate has not yet been ordered to be distributed. Please provide a (revised) proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 11/5/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The initial petition filed on 5/26/23 had not clearly set forth whether Anita H. Sanchez was survived by issue.) NB: All pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party who is appearing (petitioning), e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BUGEJA 2024 FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer To Proper Venue As To Petitioners Contest And Petition To Invalidate The Bugeja 2024 Family Trust And Associated Pour-Over Wills And Title Transfers
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the court is Ashley Reed's Motion to Transfer to Proper Venue as to Petitioners' Contest and Petition to Invalidate The Bugeja 2024 Family Trust and Associated Pour-Over Wills and Title Transfers ("Motion to Transfer"). Movant's request for judicial notice of Petitioners' Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters of Administration, for Authorization to Administer Under the Independent Administration of Estates Act filed on April 1, 2025 in Estate of Bruna Loretta Bugeja (PES-25-308367) and Will of Peter P. Bugeja, executed on August 3, 2024 and lodged on December 13, 2024 in case number PDW-24-062875 is GRANTED, pursuant to Evid. Code section 452(d). The Motion to Transfer is DENIED without prejudice, for the following reasons. Probate Code section 17005 provides guidance on the proper country for commencement of a proceeding challenging a trust under section 17000 et seq. Section 17005 provides that proper venue for such a dispute is as follows: (a)(1) In the case of a living trust, the county where the principal place of administration of the trust is located. (2) In the case of a testamentary trust, either the county where the decedent's estate is administered or where the principal place of administration of the trust is located. (b) If a living trust has no trustee, the proper county for commencement of a proceeding for appointing a trustee is the county where the trust property, or some portion of the trust property, is located. (c) Except as otherwise provided in subdivisions (a) and (b), the proper county for commencement of a proceeding pursuant to this division is determined by the rules applicable to civil actions generally. The Movant contends she is the successor trustee of the instant trust and that Movant's day-to-day administration of the trust occurs at her residence in Placer County. Based on these contentions, she argues that the court should grant the Motion to Transfer this matter to Placer County Superior Court pursuant to subsection (a)(1). However, this matter presents other variables and factors for the court's consideration which run counter to a mechanical application of section 17005(a)(1) on the basis of Movant's assertion that she is the trustee of the instant trust.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BUGEJA 2024 FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] The court also finds that it is not legally insignificant that Petitioners filed an unchallenged Notice of Related Case in both matters pursuant to California Rules of Court, rule 3.300, prior to Movant filing the instant Motion to Transfer. On May 19, 2025, Movant filed a motion in the estate matter to transfer venue to Placer County. The court heard and subsequently denied the motion to transfer venue, and by order entered June 23, 2025, held that San Francisco was the proper county for administration of Decedent's estate. On May 23, 2025, Petitioners filed Notice of Related Case in this trust matter, referencing the estate matter. The attached Proof of Service states service by mail the same day. The Notice alleges the trust matter and estate matter (1) involve the same parties and are based on the same or similar claims; (2) arise from the same or substantially identical transactions, incidents, or events requiring the determination of the same or substantially identical questions of law or fact; (3) involves claims against, title to, possession of, or damages to the same property and is likely for other reasons to require substantial duplication of judicial resources if heard by different judges. Under Rule 3.300(G), a party must file opposition to the notice within five days after service. "The response must state why one or more of the cases listed in the notice are not related or why other good cause exists for the court not to transfer the cases to or from a particular court or department." No opposition to the Notice was filed within the statutory period. As stated in Harris v. Rojas (2021) 66 Cal. App. 5th 817, 820, "The notice [of related case] allows the trial court to promote efficiency, to avoid duplicative effort, to combat judge-shopping, and to minimize the prospect of conflicting results. The trial court system relies on this mechanism to detect and to cure the problem of parallel litigation within its jurisdiction." The court finds these interests present and compelling in ruling on this Motion. Here, the court finds that the trust and estate matters are legally and factually related. Neither party argues otherwise. Further, although not fulsomely addressed in the moving or opposition papers, the court finds judicial economy and avoidance of potentially inconsistent factual or legal findings being issued by two separate courts attempting to resolve these interrelated cases are compelling reasons for both matters to remain in this court. Petitioners shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BUGEJA 2024 FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] The verified Trust Contest and Petition facially alleges at paragraph 4 the basis for venue in San Francisco County, as follows: The place of administration for the Bugeja 2004 Family Trust at all times herein mentioned, is San Francisco, California. The initial trustees, decedents Peter and Bruna Bugeja, were residents of the City and County of San Francisco and kept their financial and property records at their home at 363 Harvard Street in San Francisco. Their financial and bank accounts were maintained in San Francisco and part of their real estate was situated in San Francisco. Upon the death of the surviving spouse, Bruna Bugeja, on January 19, 2025, the alleged trust became irrevocable. Since Section 3.03 of the Bugeja 2004 Family Trust instrument expressly bars a related person "within the meaning of Internal Revenue Code Section 672(c)" from being trustee, the trust has no trustee. Since part of the trust property - 363 Harvard Street - is situated in San Francisco, venue is in the County of San Francisco by reason of Probate Code section 17005(b). Petitioner's argument that there is a vacancy in the office of trustee following the death of surviving settlor Bruna Bugeja under the terms of the Trust is reasserted in the Opposition to the Motion to Transfer. The Reply responds to this argument with citation to separate language in the Trust, on which Movant asserts that she is now the successor trustee. The court will not make a finding on the basis of this Motion regarding the proper or correct interpretation of the Trust language at issue, or rule on the competing allegations regarding whether the role of trustee is or is not vacant. Instead, relevant to this Motion to Transfer Venue, the court notes Petitioners allege there is a vacancy in the office of trustee. Under Probate Code section 17005(b), "If a living trust has no trustee, the proper county for commencement of a proceeding for appointing a trustee is the county where the trust property, or some portion of the trust property, is located." The parties do not contest that real property listed on Schedule C of the contested trust instrument includes real property located in San Francisco. Analysis under "the rules applicable to civil actions generally" also supports the trust matter remaining in this court (see, Prob. C. section 17005(c)). Here, the gravamen of the trust petition is to contest the validity of the trust instrument and related pour-over wills and determine the proper ownership of the real properties and other assets listed on Schedule C of the contested trust instrument. Petitioners' pleadings seek to invalidate the contested trust instrument and pour-over wills and recover Decedent's real and personal property into her estate. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 392(a)(1), "[T]he superior court in the county where the real property that is the subject of the action, or some part thereof, is situated, is the proper court for the trial of [ ] the recovery of real property, or of an estate or interest therein, or for the determination in any form, of that right or interest, and for injuries to real property." Under Code of Civil Procedure section 395.1, if a party "is sued in an official or representative capacity as executor, administrator, guardian, conservator, or trustee on a claim for the payment of money or for the recovery of personal property, the action must be tried in the county that has jurisdiction over the estate the defendant represents." The parties do not contest that real property listed on Schedule C of the contested trust instrument includes real property located in San Francisco. This court has already determined in the related estate matter has been properly filed in this county.
